message:
fr-conformance: add array expected-frame support for WAITAOF tuple assertions (bd-1nuq)

Why:
- Packet-006 replication conformance needed to assert WAITAOF's tuple reply shape directly in fixture expectations.
- ExpectedFrame previously supported only scalar/simple/bulk responses, so WAITAOF fixture coverage could not encode array outputs.

What changed:
- Added ExpectedFrame::Array { value: Vec<ExpectedFrame> } to crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs.
- Extended expected_to_frame to recursively map array expectations into RESP arrays.
- Added unit test expected_frame_array_kind_deserializes_and_maps_to_resp_array to lock JSON decoding + frame mapping behavior.
- Updated crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/fr_p2c_006_replication_journey.json with a WAITAOF case (WAITAOF 1 0 0) expecting [1,0], and shifted subsequent fixture timestamps.

Validation:
- cargo fmt --all && cargo fmt --check
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
- rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-conformance -- --nocapture
- ubs --only=rust crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs (reports pre-existing high-volume findings in this long-lived file; no new compiler/test failures from this slice)

Traceability:
- Bead: bd-1nuq
- Mail thread: br-1nuq
